summary: I compiled the two Review-phase critic verdicts into a single PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. To do this faithfully I read both critic verdicts (`requirements-conformance-critic` → **pass** tracing all 34 requirements to evidence; `code-quality-critic` → **needs_work** with one major and two minor findings), then cross-checked the git history and current code to establish ordering: the code-quality findings (F1 restart step-skipping, F2 feedback duplication, F3 missing Define-phase pause guard) were sent back to Build, and implementer iteration 3 fixed all three on the current HEAD — which I verified directly in `restart_define.rb` (`next_iteration` now spans all worker steps; `.uniq` on carried feedback) and `pause.rb` (`define_phase?` guard), with the Build test-critic passing 238 tests and clean rubocop. The report covers what was asked, what was built (new/modified file inventory with the `paused`-status architecture), evidence of conformance, each finding with its resolution, the documented accepted trade-offs, and a bottom-line "ready to merge" verdict since no open blocking findings remain.
